macro_events.event_summary: G7 finance ministers convene over warnings about severe economic fallout from a prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure, implying ongoing disruption to ~20% of global seaborne oil and ~20% of LNG trade.
primary_drivers: Oil and LNG supply shock from blocked Persian Gulf chokepoint drives crude/gas prices higher
Tanker rerouting and scarcity of available vessels spikes shipping day rates
Energy-driven inflation re-acceleration pressures central banks and risk assets

beneficiaries.rationale: Diamondback Energy is a pure-play Permian US onshore producer with zero Hormuz transit exposure; realized prices rise directly with WTI/Brent spike while production is unaffected.
EOG Resources is a low-cost US onshore E&P; every $10 sustained crude move translates roughly linearly to free cash flow given no Middle East operational footprint.

Cheniere Energy is the largest US LNG exporter; with ~20% of global LNG transiting Hormuz (Qatari cargoes) blocked, US Gulf Coast LNG becomes the marginal replacement supply and spot/contract margins widen.
Flex LNG operates LNG carriers on spot/short-term charters; rerouting Qatari volumes plus tight global LNG shipping capacity drives charter rates sharply higher.

Scorpio Tankers (product tankers) benefits from refined product dislocations as Middle East refineries (Ruwais, Ras Tanura) are stranded and product must be sourced from longer-haul Atlantic basin routes — ton-miles surge.
International Seaways operates VLCCs/Suezmaxes; with Gulf crude flow disrupted, available tonnage outside the Gulf commands premium rates and Atlantic basin tanker day rates spike on rerouting.
ExxonMobil has Permian/Guyana production unaffected by Hormuz, while its integrated trading and refining arms profit from crude price dislocations and crack spread widening.

losers.rationale: Delta Air Lines has jet fuel as ~25-30% of operating costs and limited hedging; a sustained crude spike from Hormuz closure directly compresses margins with weak ability to pass through via fares in summer travel season.
United Airlines similarly exposed to jet fuel cost shock; also has higher international/long-haul mix where rerouted flights around Middle East add fuel burn.
Carnival Cruise burns bunker fuel and faces discretionary travel demand destruction as recession fears from G7 warnings dampen booking trends.
Dow Inc uses natural gas and naphtha as primary feedstocks; spiking global energy prices compress chemical margins, and weakening industrial demand from inflation shock hits volumes.
Walmart faces diesel/transport cost inflation across its supply chain plus consumer wallet squeeze as gasoline prices rise — historically a clear headwind to discretionary basket size.
General Motors faces double hit: consumer demand destruction from gas price spike (worst for trucks/SUVs which are highest-margin) plus input cost pressure from energy-intensive steel and logistics.

headlines: G7 finance ministers to meet amid warning of economic consequences of prolonged Strait of Hormuz closure
